summary: Foundation layout QA now includes all-walkable reachability, opened the isolated right-side room with a 5-tile entry, and moved the exit to the bottom-right corner opposite spawn.
observations: The 45 x 45 foundation map remains a placeholder layout.
validate-levels now checks bounded row/column segments below 3 tiles.
validate-levels now checks diagonal solid-corner cuts that create one-tile squeeze paths.
The detected diagonal squeeze at rows 29/30, columns 17/18 was widened by opening row 30 columns 16-17.
User-reported world coordinates 12.1, -2.1 and 12.2, 7.6 now map to solid wall cells instead of open edge notches.
validate-levels now checks cardinal-wall plus diagonal-corner pinch patterns.
The previous corner-pinch pass had 1640 walkable tiles and remained spawn-to-exit reachable.
Added minEntryUnits 5 to catch door-like structural wall-band breaks that are too narrow for the player plus weapon footprint.
Widened the top vertical divider entry at column 22 rows 4-8.
Widened row 20 entries at columns 5-9 and 28-32.
Widened the row 30 right entry at columns 36-40.
Added minEntrySplitSideUnits 3 and minEntrySplitAreaUnits 6 to catch continuing wall posts that split structural entries into narrow branches.
User-reported world coordinates -10.0, 17.8; -9.4, 17.2; and 8.2, 17.5 mapped to the bottom row 39 wall-band entries.
Widened row 30 first entry to columns 15-22.
Widened row 39 entries to columns 8-14 and 26-32.
The prior foundation map had 1653 walkable tiles and remained spawn-to-exit reachable before the isolated-room access pass.
The foundation level now uses steel-toned Kenney CC0 prototype textures for floor, wall, and roof surfaces.
A full-level roof plane covers the 45 x 45 foundation arena at 3.92 units high.
Gameplay-height screenshot QA confirmed the steel floor, wall, and roof readability after waiting for GPU texture upload.
The isolated room at rows 11-19, columns 34-43 was unreachable from spawn and contained enemy marker row 13 column 38 plus cover row 15 column 39.
Opened column 33 rows 12-16 to create a 5-tile structural entry into the room.
Moved the X exit marker from row 1 column 43 to row 43 column 43, opposite spawn at row 1 column 1.
validate-levels now rejects any non-solid tile that is unreachable from spawn.
The current foundation map has 1658 walkable tiles and all are reachable from spawn.
decisions: Use unique external kits per level.
Define level-relative anchors for powerups and encounters.
Keep the foundation level minimum passage gate at 3 tiles.
Reject one-tile corner pinches even when long straight runs still pass.
Keep general traversal lanes at 3 tiles but require structural wall-band entries to be at least 5 tiles.
Reject continuing wall posts that split structural entries when either side is below 3 tiles or both sides total below 6 tiles.
Treat the Kenney prototype textures as lightweight foundation placeholders until a level-specific kit is selected.
Reject unreachable walkable rooms, enemies, cover-adjacent floor, and exit spaces.
Keep the foundation exit in the bottom-right interior corner opposite the top-left spawn unless the objective changes.
caughtIssues: The previous lane QA could pass a diagonal wall-corner squeeze that still looked like a one-unit path.
The straight-run validator could pass L-shaped edge notches because their horizontal and vertical runs were both long.
The corner-pinch validator could not catch straight 2- or 3-tile door-like entries with no diagonal pinch pattern.
A 5-tile structural entry can still feel like two 2-tile branches when a wall post sits just inside the entry.
Early texture screenshots could capture before GPU upload and look blank/white; browser smoke should wait for renderer texture count before validating the frame.
Spawn-to-exit reachability did not catch an isolated room with an enemy and cover.
risks: Weak scale references can make buildings, cover, and enemies feel wrong on mobile.
Future prop placement can reintroduce narrow diagonal cuts if it bypasses validate-levels.
